productInvariants.label: Browser-first
productInvariants.description: Core browsing must remain usable when OpenClaw is unavailable.

productInvariants.label: Tab/Thread/Session Separation
productInvariants.description: Tab, thread, and OpenClaw session remain distinct concepts and may evolve independently.

productInvariants.label: Main-process Authority
productInvariants.description: Persisted runtime state belongs to the main process; renderer code subscribes and dispatches.

productInvariants.label: Text-first Context
productInvariants.description: Structured text snapshots remain the primary browser context contract, with richer tools layered on top.

criticalFlows.description: Create, activate, navigate, reload, and close tabs without AI.
Threads survive navigation, refresh, restore, and pageKey reuse rules.
Local OpenClaw discovery, model sync, and binding bootstrap remain recoverable.
Ask, summarize, explain-selection, and skill assist fallback all complete with visible trace.
Background handoff writes a task record and can be observed by the renderer.
GenTab creation, pending metadata, generation, completion, and retry all use one consistent contract.
History, bookmarks, downloads, diagnostics, settings, and skills surfaces continue to behave like tab content.

architectureRules.reason: The engineering system must stay documented in-repo.
Critical flows need a stable acceptance matrix.
Iteration rules must be explicit and versioned.
Browser/OpenClaw boundaries need an explicit in-repo architecture baseline.
Browser context should be packaged as a stable Sabrina runtime contract.
Browser skill compatibility should be explicit via registry or metadata, not heuristic only.
GenTab host execution should compose browser context packaging through a focused service.
The tracked local agent task IPC handler must call the real task helper.
GenTab must use the runLocalAgentTurn string message contract.
runLocalAgentTurn returns text, not content.
Task store events must be subscribable from the renderer.
Thread message persistence must flow through an explicit main-process IPC contract.
GenTab completion must be observable by the renderer.
GenTab must persist pending metadata before opening the internal tab.
Renderer should not do routine durable thread writeback after main-process authority was introduced.
Thread durable state should live in main-process storage, not renderer localStorage.
Renderer should consume thread runtime projections, not run resolver logic locally.
Chat commands should send turn intent only; page context extraction belongs to main.
Renderer should subscribe to thread runtime-state only after legacy thread contract cleanup.
TabManager should emit browser snapshots only; tab-thread binding belongs to a separate coordinator.
Renderer should project OpenClaw runtime state, not carry a duplicated binding setup workflow tree.
OpenClaw model state should advance in main and flow back to renderer as projection.
OpenClawStateStore should persist and broadcast only; snapshot fan-out belongs to a separate service.
OpenClaw IPC wiring should be isolated from the generic Electron handler hub.
Thread page-key and host-label helpers should come from one shared implementation, not drift between runtime and renderer.
Thread store should receive host file-system support by injection, not import Electron directly.
Thread store should report through injected host adapters, not a hardwired Electron monitor import.
Browser library persistence should not pull Electron directly into the store layer.
Browser library store should emit diagnostics through injected host support only.
OpenClaw task store should not import Electron directly once host adapters are introduced.
OpenClaw task store should report through injected host support, not a static monitoring import.
OpenClaw state store should be host-fed and persist through adapters, not direct Electron imports.
OpenClaw state store should not import host monitoring directly after adapter extraction.
Browser IPC registration should live in a dedicated host module instead of the top-level IPC hub.
Thread IPC registration should be isolated from the top-level IPC hub.
GenTab IPC registration should be isolated from the top-level IPC hub.
OpenClaw model policy should live in a focused service instead of the facade.
OpenClaw pairing approvals should live in a focused service instead of the facade.
Workspace memory projection should live in a focused service instead of the facade.
GenTab prompt and normalization logic should live in a dedicated browser-domain service.
Browser AI prompt construction should live in its own service instead of the page extraction module.
Tab context snapshotting belongs to the browser runtime domain, not the Electron host folder.
Browser URL helpers should come from one focused browser-domain service.
Thread IPC execution logic should be split from the registration module.

architectureRules.pattern: 你是 Sabrina 的 GenTab 生成器
architectureRules.reason: GenTab IPC registration should not embed the full generation prompt builder inline.
GenTab IPC registration should delegate normalization to a focused service.
Native browser menu UI belongs to the Electron host layer.
Browser runtime should receive Electron guest-view support through a dedicated host adapter.
Host main should import native browser menu UI from host/electron, not runtime/browser.
Browser IPC handlers should import native menu UI from host/electron.
Browser tab runtime should receive Electron guest-view creation through injected host support.
Browser tab runtime should not import host monitoring directly after host adapter extraction.
Browser tab runtime should not resolve host preload paths directly.
GenTab host orchestration should not depend on the tab manager's internal Map shape.
GenTab host orchestration should pull tab context from the browser runtime domain, not a host-local wrapper.
Thread IPC should depend on explicit tab-runtime selectors, not the full active-tab object.
Thread IPC registration should not inline the AI action execution flow.
Thread IPC registration should not inline OpenClaw task recording logic.
Thread IPC registration should consume browser tab context through runtime/browser.
ThreadTurnService should consume a browser context package instead of rebuilding attachment resolution inline.
AI action routing should consume the Sabrina browser context package contract.
GenTab generation should consume the Sabrina browser context package contract.
Browser ask prompts should be built from the packaged browser context contract.
Browser skill prompts should be built from the packaged browser context contract.
Browser skill compatibility must consult explicit registry or metadata before heuristics.
URL-native browser skills should resolve explicit source routing instead of only checking isHttpUrl.
Page context extraction should stay separate from browser AI prompt construction.
Browser library store should not own shared URL label helpers.
Browser library store should keep its record helper utilities private.
Tab runtime should not expose raw tab-serialization helpers as public API.
Startup tab configuration should stay internal to the tab runtime.
Address normalization should stay private until another browser-domain caller truly needs it.
Tab runtime should keep its tab registry private and expose only focused selectors.
Tab runtime should not export raw active-tab objects once host-facing selectors are available.
Tab runtime should not export raw tab objects by id when focused selectors exist.
Legacy shim directory should not be bundled once host/runtime paths are the canonical entrypoints.
Debug packaging should not keep including the removed shim directory.
